title: Preconditioner (Z^TZ+λI)⊗K violates the O(M) ban — use observed Gram

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G2 (central, Lemma L4).** The preconditioner suggested in prior strategy notes, $\mathcal P=(Z^TZ+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$, requires forming $Z^TZ$, which costs $O(Mr^2)$ — **order $M$, forbidden** by the problem ("avoid any computation of order $N$ and order $M$").

An admissible preconditioner must be built from observed quantities only, e.g. the observed Gram $\tilde Z^T\tilde Z$ (cost $O(qr^2)$), giving $\mathcal P=(\tilde Z^T\tilde Z+\lambda I_r)\otimes K$, or a block-Jacobi / diagonal preconditioner assembled from $\tilde K,\tilde Z$.

**Required:** (a) specify an $O(M)$-free preconditioner; (b) prove it is SPD (needs $K\succ0$, $\lambda>0$); (c) give its application cost, including any one-time factorizations (Cholesky $K=LL^T$ at $O(n^3)$, and the $r\times r$ Gram factorization). Repeated verifier failures are plausibly tied to this $O(M)$ conflation.
